title: audit — developer configuration
description: Deploy-static configuration. Mirrors `config/audit.php`. Governs storage adaptation (table name), retention windows (default + max years, hot-tier days), backfill batch tuning, export format + archive bucket, and per-tenant PII-redaction opt-in. Wraps + extends the vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` config; keys that overlap are intentionally repeated at our namespace so a change here does not require touching the vendor's `config/audit.php`.

keys.description: Must match `owen-it.laravel-auditing.audits_table_name`. Kept in sync at boot; drift throws AuditConfigDrift at bootstrap.
Owned table for tenant-scoped retention overrides.

retention.description: Retention windows for the vendor-adapted `audits` table.

keys.description: Baseline retention for every tenant. SOX §404 + IRS + HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)(i) alignment.
Cap for `AuditRetentionPolicy.retention_years`. Enterprise-only via `audit_retention_extended` entitlement.
Number of days rows stay in the primary Postgres `audits` table before ExportAuditsToArchiveJob moves them to cold storage (Enterprise + tenant-scoped).
Same as hot_days by default. Separate key so config can tune the two independently later.
Emit AuditRetentionExtensionExpiringSoonNotification when `override_ends_at - now() <= this many days`.
Row count per backfill batch. Larger = faster wall-clock but more lock contention on the `audits` heap.
Destination column receiving the backfilled value.
Primary JSONB path to read the tenant id from. Fallback path applies for deletion rows where new_values is empty.
Fallback path when new_values.tenant_id is missing (typical for delete events).
Pause between batches to keep the backfill kind to hot-path reads.
Export payload format. jsonl is the default — one JSON object per line, gzip-compressed.
Environment variable naming the destination S3 bucket. Configured per-environment (Doppler) — the bucket is regulator-controlled + separate from application storage.
Signed-URL expiry on the export's download link. 24h balances 'DPO has time to grab it' with 'link doesn't stay live indefinitely'.
Cap per export request. Exports exceeding this are chunked.
Include the request IP on each audit row. Regulators typically require this; disabled only under a legal-privacy exception.
Include the request User-Agent on each audit row.

Off by default — regulators need full diff context to reconstruct the mutation. Tenants may opt in via settings.audit_redact_pii; when on, a registered PiiRedactor runs against old_values + new_values at save time before persistence.
When true, PurgeExpiredAuditsJob writes a summary `audit.purged { tenant_id, cutoff_at, row_count }` audit row. Off only in test environments.

immutability.description: Immutability-enforcement toggles. All defaults ON in every environment except unit-test overrides.

keys.description: AuditObserver::updating throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROW_IMMUTABLE when true.
AuditObserver::deleting refuses unless caller is the PurgeExpiredAuditsJob service account.
audit.enforce_read_only middleware refuses non-GET methods on every /audits* route.

vendor.description: Vendor `owen-it/laravel-auditing` alignment. These keys mirror vendor config so the two stay in sync at boot.

keys.description: Mirrors vendor `audit.enabled`. Disabling turns off capture globally (emergency kill switch — pair with the `audit.capture` feature flag).
Vendor-recognized event names captured by default. Custom events register at boot via the vendor `Auditable::registerAuditableTransition` seam.
Vendor `audit.threshold` — max audit rows retained PER auditable model instance (0 = unlimited). We keep 0 because retention is time-based, not per-instance-count-based.
Vendor `audit.strict` — restrict which model attributes are auditable to only the model's $auditInclude list (safer default).
When true, PurgeExpiredAuditsJob logs the intended cutoff but does not delete. Test envs only.
Relax the update-forbid guard for tests that must fabricate audit rows in a specific state. Never in production.
